diagram_name: Semiconductor Lattice Constant and Bandgap Map
learning_objective: Students will compare lattice constants and bandgaps of common semiconductors and evaluate (Evaluate, L5) lattice mismatch implications for heterostructure design.
specification:
Type: MicroSim **sim-id:** lattice-bandgap-map
**Library:** Chart.js
**Status:** Specified **Learning objective:** Students will compare lattice constants and bandgaps of common semiconductors and evaluate (Evaluate, L5) lattice mismatch implications for heterostructure design. **Canvas size:** 780 × 500 px, responsive. **Chart type:** Scatter plot (Chart.js Scatter). Each semiconductor is a labeled data point. **Dataset:** Plot the following (lattice constant in Å on x-axis, bandgap in eV on y-axis): - Si: (5.431, 1.12), Ge: (5.658, 0.66), GaAs: (5.653, 1.42), AlAs: (5.661, 2.16), InP: (5.869, 1.35), InAs: (6.058, 0.36), GaP: (5.451, 2.26), AlP: (5.467, 2.45), InSb: (6.479, 0.17), GaSb: (6.096, 0.72). - Color direct-gap materials green (#4caf50), indirect-gap red (#f44336). - Draw curved interpolation lines between ternary alloy endpoints (e.g., GaAs-AlAs in gray, InAs-GaAs in orange) labeled with the alloy name. **Interaction:** - Clicking or hovering a data point displays an infobox: material name, lattice constant, bandgap, gap type (direct/indirect), and key applications. - A vertical reference line at x = 5.431 Å (Si) is toggleable via a "Show Si reference" checkbox. - A vertical reference line at x = 5.869 Å (InP) is toggleable via "Show InP reference" checkbox. **Implementation:** Chart.js v4, single HTML file. Custom tooltip callbacks for rich infoboxes. Parent canvas to document.querySelector('main').
